volumeTitle: Antigone. The Women of Trachis. Philoctetes. Oedipus at Colonus
label: Antigone (Section 160 - Section 330)

sections.label: Section 160
sections.original: ὅτι σύγκλητον τήνδε γερόντων
sections.translation: that he has proposed this special conference of elders , and summoned it by a general mandate ?

sections.label: Section 162
sections.original: ἄνδρες , τὰ μὲν δὴ πόλεος ἀσφαλῶς θεοὶ
sections.translation: My fellow citizens ! First , the gods , after tossing the fate of our city on wild waves , have once more righted it . Second , I have ordered you through my messengers to come here

sections.label: Section 165
sections.original: ἔστειλʼ ἱκέσθαι τοῦτο μὲν τὰ Λαΐου
sections.translation: apart from all the rest , because I knew , first of all , how constant was your reverence for the power of the throne of Laius ; how , again , you were reverent , when Oedipus was guiding our city ; and lastly , how , when he was dead , you still maintained loyal thoughts towards his children .

sections.label: Section 170
sections.original: ὅτʼ οὖν ἐκεῖνοι πρὸς διπλῆς μοίρας μίαν
sections.translation: Since , then , these latter have fallen in one day by a twofold doom — each striking , each struck , both with the stain of a brother ’ s murder — I now possess all the power and the throne according to my kinship with the dead .

sections.label: Section 175
sections.original: ἀμήχανον δὲ παντὸς ἀνδρὸς ἐκμαθεῖν
sections.translation: Now , it is impossible to know fully any man ’ s character , will , or judgment , until he has been proved by the test of rule and law-giving . For if anyone who directs the entire city does not cling to the best and wisest plans ,

sections.label: Section 180
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἐκ φόβου του γλῶσσαν ἐγκλῄσας ἔχει ,
sections.translation: but because of some fear keeps his lips locked , then , in my judgment , he is and has long been the most cowardly traitor . And if any man thinks a friend more important than his fatherland , that man , I say , is of no account . Zeus , god who sees all things always , be my witness —

sections.label: Section 185
sections.original: οὔτʼ ἂν σιωπήσαιμι τὴν ἄτην ὁρῶν
sections.translation: I would not be silent if I saw ruin , instead of safety , marching upon the citizens . Nor would I ever make a man who is hostile to my country a friend to myself , because I know this , that our country is the ship that bears us safe , and that only when

sections.label: Section 190
sections.original: πλέοντες ὀρθῆς τοὺς φίλους ποιούμεθα .
sections.translation: we sail her on a straight course can we make true friends . Such are the rules by which I strengthen this city . Akin to these is the edict which I have now published to the citizenry concerning the sons of Oedipus : Eteocles , who fell fighting

sections.label: Section 195
sections.original: ὄλωλε τῆσδε , πάντʼ ἀριστεύσας δόρει ,
sections.translation: in behalf of our city and who excelled all in battle , they shall entomb and heap up every sacred offering that descends to the noblest of the dead below . But as for his brother , Polyneices , I mean , who on his return from exile wanted to burn to the ground

sections.label: Section 200
sections.original: φυγὰς κατελθὼν ἠθέλησε μὲν πυρὶ
sections.translation: the city of his fathers and his race ’ s gods , and wanted to feed on kindred blood and lead the remnant into slavery — it has been proclaimed to the city that no one shall give him funeral honors or lamentation ,

sections.label: Section 205
sections.original: ἐᾶν δʼ ἄθαπτον καὶ πρὸς οἰωνῶν δέμας
sections.translation: but all must leave him unburied and a sight of shame , with his body there for birds and dogs to eat . This is my will , and never will I allow the traitor to stand in honor before the just . But whoever has good will to Thebes ,

sections.label: Section 210
sections.original: καὶ ζῶν ὁμοίως ἐξ ἐμοῦ τιμήσεται .
sections.translation: he shall be honored by me in death as in life .

sections.label: Section 211
sections.original: σοὶ ταῦτʼ ἀρέσκει , παῖ Μενοικέως Κρέον ,
sections.translation: That is your will , Creon , towards this city ’ s enemy and its friend . And the power is yours , I believe , to make use of every law whatsoever , both concerning the dead and all us who live .

sections.label: Section 215
sections.original: ὡς ἂν σκοποὶ νῦν εἶτε τῶν εἰρημένων .
sections.translation: See , then , that you be guardians of my commands .

sections.label: Section 216
sections.original: νεωτέρῳ τῳ τοῦτο βαστάζειν πρόθες .
sections.translation: Lay the weight of this task on some younger man .

sections.label: Section 217
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εἴσʼ ἕτοιμοι τοῦ νεκροῦ γʼ ἐπίσκοποι .
sections.translation: That is not what I meant — the guards for the corpse are already in place .

sections.label: Section 218
sections.original: τί δῆτʼ ἂν ἄλλο τοῦτʼ ἐπεντέλλοις ἔτι ;
sections.translation: Then what is this other command that you would give ?

sections.label: Section 219
sections.original: τὸ μὴ ʼπιχωρεῖν τοῖς ἀπιστοῦσιν τάδε .
sections.translation: That you not give way to the breakers of my commands .

sections.label: Section 220
sections.original: οὔκ ἔστιν οὕτω μῶρος ὃς θανεῖν ἐρᾷ .
sections.translation: There is no one so foolish as to crave death .

sections.label: Section 221
sections.original: καὶ μὴν ὁ μισθός γʼ , οὗτος · ἀλλʼ ὑπʼ ἐλπίδων
sections.translation: I assure you , that is the wage for disobedience . Yet by just the hope of it , money has many times corrupted men .

sections.label: Section 223
sections.original: ἄναξ , ἐρῶ μὲν οὐχ ὅπως τάχους ὕπο
sections.translation: My king , I will not say that I arrive breathless because of speed , or from the action of a swift foot .

sections.label: Section 225
sections.original: πολλὰς γὰρ ἔσχον φροντίδων ἐπιστάσεις ,
sections.translation: For often I brought myself to a stop because of my thoughts , and wheeled round in my path to return . My mind was telling me many things : Fool , why do you go to where your arrival will mean your punishment ? Idiot , are you dallying again ? If Creon learns it from another , must you not suffer for it ?

sections.label: Section 230
sections.original: ἄλλου παρʼ ἀνδρός ; πῶς σὺ δῆτʼ οὐκ ἀλγύνει ;
sections.translation: So debating , I made my way unhurriedly , slow , and thus a short road was made long . At last , however , the view prevailed that I should come here — to you . Even if my report brings no good , still will I tell you ,

sections.label: Section 235
sections.original: τῆς ἐλπίδος γὰρ ἔρχομαι δεδραγμένος ,
sections.translation: since I come with a good grip on one hope , that I can suffer nothing except what is my fate .

sections.label: Section 237
sections.original: τί δʼ ἐστὶν ἀνθʼ οὗ τήνδʼ ἔχεις ἀθυμίαν ;
sections.translation: And what is it that so disheartens you ?

sections.label: Section 238
sections.original: φράσαι θέλω σοι πρῶτα τἀμαυτοῦ · τὸ γὰρ
sections.translation: I want to tell you first about myself — I did not do the deed , nor did I see the doer ,

sections.label: Section 240
sections.original: οὐδʼ ἂν δικαίως ἐς κακὸν πέσοιμί τι .
sections.translation: so it would be wrong that I should come to any harm .

sections.label: Section 241
sections.original: εὖ γε στοχάζει κἀποφάργνυσαι κύκλῳ
sections.translation: Like a bowman you aim well at your target from a distance , and all around you hedge yourself off well from the deed . It is clear that you have some unheard-of thing to tell .

sections.label: Section 243
sections.original: τὰ δεινὰ γάρ τοι προστίθησʼ ὄκνον πολύν .
sections.translation: That I do , for terrible news imposes great hesitation .

sections.label: Section 244
sections.original: οὔκουν ἐρεῖς ποτʼ , εἶτʼ ἀπαλλαχθεὶς ἄπει ;
sections.translation: Then tell it , will you , and so unburdened go away ?

sections.label: Section 245
sections.original: καὶ δὴ λέγω σοι . τὸν νεκρόν τις ἀρτίως
sections.translation: Well , here it is . The corpse — some one has just given it burial and disappeared after sprinkling thirsty dust on the flesh and performing the other rites that piety demands .

sections.label: Section 248
sections.original: τί φής ; τίς ἀνδρῶν ἦν ὁ τολμήσας τάδε ;
sections.translation: What are you saying ? What man dared do this ?

sections.label: Section 249
sections.original: οὐκ οἶδʼ · ἐκεῖ γὰρ οὔτε του γενῇδος ἦν
sections.translation: I do not know . For there was no scar of a pickax to be seen there ,

sections.label: Section 250
sections.original: πλῆγμʼ , οὐ δικέλλης ἐκβολή . στύφλος δὲ γῆ
sections.translation: no earth thrown up by a mattock . The ground was hard and dry , unbroken , not rolled over by wheels . The doer was someone who left no trace . When the first day-watchman showed it to us , a discomforting amazement fell on us all .

sections.label: Section 255
sections.original: ὁ μὲν γὰρ ἠφάνιστο , τυμβήρης μὲν οὔ ,
sections.translation: The dead man was veiled from us — not shut within a tomb , but a light cover of dust was on him , as if put there by the hand of one who shunned a curse . And no sign was visible that any beast of prey or any dog had approached or torn him . Then evil words flew thick and loud among us ,

sections.label: Section 260
sections.original: φύλαξ ἐλέγχων φύλακα , κἂν ἐγίγνετο
sections.translation: guard accusing guard . It would even have come to blows in the end , nor was there anyone there to prevent it : every man was the culprit , and no one was plainly guilty , while all disclaimed knowledge of the act . We were ready to take red-hot iron in our hands ,

sections.label: Section 265
sections.original: καὶ πῦρ διέρπειν καὶ θεοὺς ὁρκωμοτεῖν ,
sections.translation: to walk through fire and to swear oaths by the gods that we had neither done the deed , nor shared knowledge of the planning or the doing . At last , when our investigating got us nowhere , someone spoke up and made us all bend our faces

sections.label: Section 270
sections.original: νεῦσαι φόβῳ προὔτρεψεν · οὐ γὰρ εἴχομεν
sections.translation: in fear towards the earth . For we did not know how we could argue with him , nor yet prosper , if we did what he said . His argument was that the deed must be reported to you and not hidden . This view prevailed , and so it was that

sections.label: Section 275
sections.original: πάλος καθαιρεῖ τοῦτο τἀγαθὸν λαβεῖν .
sections.translation: the lot doomed miserable me to win this prize . So here I stand , as unwelcome to you as I am unwilling , I well know . For no man delights in the bearer of bad news .

sections.label: Section 278
sections.original: ἄναξ , ἐμοί τοί , μή τι καὶ θεήλατον
sections.translation: My king , my thoughts have long been deliberating whether this deed is somehow the work of gods ?

sections.label: Section 280
sections.original: παῦσαι , πρὶν ὀργῆς καὶ ʼμὲ μεστῶσαι λέγων ,
sections.translation: Quiet , before your words truly fill me with rage , so that you not be found at the same time foolish as well as old . You say what is intolerable when you claim that the gods have concern for that corpse . Was it in high esteem for his benefactions

sections.label: Section 285
sections.original: ἔκρυπτον αὐτόν , ὅστις ἀμφικίονας
sections.translation: that they sought to hide him , when he had come to burn their columned shrines , their sacred treasures and their land , and scatter its laws to the winds ? Or do you see the gods honoring the wicked ? It cannot be . No ! From the very first

sections.label: Section 290
sections.original: ἄνδρες μόλις φέροντες ἐρρόθουν ἐμοί ,
sections.translation: certain men of the city were chafing at this edict and muttering against me , tossing their heads in secret , and they did not keep their necks duly under the yoke in submission to me . By those men , I am certain , they were led astray and bribed to do this deed .

sections.label: Section 295
sections.original: οὐδὲν γὰρ ἀνθρώποισιν οἷον ἄργυρος
sections.translation: Nothing so evil as money ever grew to be current among men . This destroys cities , this drives men from their homes , this trains and warps honest minds to set themselves to works of shame ,

sections.label: Section 300
sections.original: πανουργίας δʼ ἔδειξεν ἀνθρώποις ἔχειν
sections.translation: this teaches people to practise villainies , and to know every act of unholiness . But all the men who did this job for hire have made sure that , sooner or later , they shall suffer the punishment . Now , as Zeus still has my reverence , know this well —

sections.label: Section 305
sections.original: εὖ τοῦτʼ ἐπίστασʼ , ὅρκιος δέ σοι λέγω ·
sections.translation: I tell you on my oath . If you do not find the very hand that made this burial , and reveal him before my eyes , mere death shall not suffice for you , not before , hung up alive , you have made this outrage plain ,

sections.label: Section 310
sections.original: ἵνʼ εἰδότες τὸ κέρδος ἔνθεν οἰστέον
sections.translation: so that hereafter you may thieve with better knowledge of where your money should be received from , and learn that it is best not to be fond of money-making from any and every source . For you will find that ill-gotten gains bring more men to ruin than to safety .

sections.label: Section 315
sections.original: εἰπεῖν τι δώσεις ἢ στραφεὶς οὕτως ἴω ;
sections.translation: Will you allow me to speak ? Or shall I just turn and go ?

sections.label: Section 316
sections.original: οὐκ οἶσθα καὶ νῦν ὡς ἀνιαρῶς λέγεις ;
sections.translation: Do you not know even now how much your voice sickens me ?

sections.label: Section 317
sections.original: ἐν τοῖσιν ὠσὶν ἢ ʼπὶ τῇ ψυχῇ δάκνει ;
sections.translation: Is the pain in your ears , or in your soul ?

sections.label: Section 318
sections.original: τί δὲ ῥυθμίζεις τὴν ἐμὴν λύπην ὅπου ;
sections.translation: And why would you define the seat of my pain ?

sections.label: Section 319
sections.original: ὁ δρῶν σʼ ἀνιᾷ τὰς φρένας , τὰ δʼ ὦτʼ ἐγώ .
sections.translation: He who did it hurts your heart , but I , your ears .

sections.label: Section 320
sections.original: οἴμʼ ὡς λάλημα δῆλον ἐκπεφυκὸς εἶ .
sections.translation: God ! How plain it is that you are a born babbler .

sections.label: Section 321
sections.original: οὔκουν τό γʼ ἔργον τοῦτο ποιήσας ποτέ .
sections.translation: Perhaps , but never the author of this action .

sections.label: Section 322
sections.original: καὶ ταῦτʼ ἐπʼ ἀργύρῳ γε τὴν ψυχὴν προδούς .
sections.translation: Yes , and what is more , you sold your life for silver .

sections.label: Section 323
sections.original: φεῦ ·
sections.translation: Ah ! It is truly sad when the judge judges wrong .

sections.label: Section 324
sections.original: κόμψευέ νυν τὴν δόξαν · εἰ δὲ ταῦτα μὴ
sections.translation: Expound on judgment as you will . But , if you fail to

sections.label: Section 325
sections.original: φανεῖτέ μοι τοὺς δρῶντας , ἐξερεῖθʼ ὅτι
sections.translation: show me the perpetrators of these crimes , you will avow that money basely earned wreaks sorrows . Exit Creon .

sections.label: Section 327
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εὑρεθείη μὲν μάλιστʼ · ἐὰν δέ τοι
sections.translation: Well , may the man be found ! That would be best . But , whether he be caught or not — for fortune must decide that — I assure you that you will not see me come here again .

sections.label: Section 330
sections.original: καὶ νῦν γὰρ ἐκτὸς ἐλπίδος γνώμης τʼ ἐμῆς
sections.translation: Saved just now beyond hope and belief , I owe the gods great thanks . Exit the Guard .
